title: Tencent Is Said to Mull Exits From Game Studios Like Marvelous
summary: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is negotiating exits from several game studio investments in Japan, including Tokyo-traded Marvelous Inc., as part of a reassessment of the company’s global portfolio, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: Greeks Get Priced Out of Summer Even After Economic Recovery
summary: Life isn’t what it used to be as a squeeze on household incomes increasingly makes long beach vacations a relic of a more prosperous past.

summary: Nissan Motor Co. shareholders rejected the reappointment of Motoo Nagai to the board, ending the tenure of an influential outside director after he lost the support of key stakeholder Renault SA, following a rare public standoff at Japan’s typically staid annual meetings.

title: Amazon Ordered to Collectively Bargain at San Francisco Warehouse
summary: A US labor board judge ruled that Amazon.com Inc. must collectively bargain with California warehouse workers, teeing up a legal battle that could end with President Donald Trump’s appointees reversing a major union-friendly precedent.

title: Citi’s Moore Says ‘Can’t Wait Too Long’ on Market Pullbacks
summary: “If you want to be longer risk, you can’t wait too long,” says Kate Moore, CIO at Citi Wealth, as she sees market pullbacks creating an opportunity to deploy capital during inflationary periods. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Steadies While Tankers Openly Enter Hormuz After Peace Deal
summary: Oil steadied while tankers become more overt in transiting the Strait of Hormuz following an agreement between Iran and the US to end their conflict.

title: North Sea Crude Weakens as Mideast Oil Floods Back to Market
summary: Key indicators in the North Sea crude market are showing signs of weakness as a wave of Middle Eastern oil heads to Europe, fueling concerns about a supply glut following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Heineken Names Coffee CEO Oliveira to Lead Turnaround
summary: Heineken NV appointed Rafael Oliveira chief executive officer, as the Dutch brewer breaks with habit by hiring an outsider to try to reverse a slump in demand.

title: Stocks Get Hit by Tech Losses as AI Worries Spread: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks slumped, with Nasdaq 100 futures falling 2.9%, as concerns about frothy valuations ignited a fresh bout of volatility following a two-month surge in risky assets. South Korea’s Kospi Index plunged 10% from a record high.

title: SpaceX’s Quickfire Investment-Grade Rating Brings Out Skeptics
summary: When Moody’s Ratings first evaluated Nvidia Corp. almost a decade ago, it settled on a Baa1 investment-grade rating, taking comfort in its relatively light debt load and more than $1 billion of free cash flow after 16 years as a public company.

title: ‘Looking Over Our Shoulder:’ Traders Are Loading Up on VIX Calls
summary: Summer is officially here for US stock traders. Recession odds aren’t alarming. The US and Iran are negotiating. Corporate America’s profits are at record highs. And trade policy? Not too much of a worry — at least for now.

title: Iran’s Race to Secure New Oil Buyers Won’t Be Easy
summary: Tehran has just a couple of months to offer its crude far and wide, and the market dynamics aren’t in its favor.

title: US Premarket Movers for June 23, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1.3% as of 7:41 a.m. in New York amid a global selloff in technology stocks.

title: Tesla Stockholders Bet on SpaceX Merger as Musk’s Real Endgame
summary: It’s been a challenging year for Tesla Inc. shareholders, losing almost 10% while watching Elon Musk’s SpaceX complete the biggest initial public offering ever and immediately become the most valuable entity in the newly minted trillionaire’s collection.

title: Iran President Visits Pakistan After Historic Deal With US
summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian arrived in Islamabad on Tuesday for talks with Pakistani officials about how to deepen the relationship between the two countries following the US-Iran truce that Pakistan helped mediate.

title: Stocks Hit by Global Tech Selloff | Bloomberg Brief 6/23/2026
summary: US equity futures slump amid a global tech rout. South Korea's Kospi Index fell 10% from a record high as investors sell off chip heavyweights such as Samsung and SK Hynix. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets and Kevin Mahn of Hennion and Walsh Asset Management discuss the AI-related volatility in markets.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan Is Running Out of Options on Yen, TD’s Bharadwaj Says
summary: Jayati Bharadwaj, head of FX strategy at TD Securities, discusses the potential for yen intervention as the currency trades near its weakest level in four decades against the dollar. She speaks on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Emerging Market Stocks Fall on Korea Selloff; Currencies Weaken
summary: A selloff in South Korea’s technology giants dragged the gauge of emerging-market equities down, while most developing-nations’ currencies weakened as global risk sentiment worsened.

title: Zerodha’s Kamath Sees Energy Transition Stocks as Key India Bet
summary: Energy transition has become a key investment theme for Nikhil Kamath, co-founder of Zerodha Broking Ltd., as the US-Iran war reinforces the sector’s importance and creates opportunities across its value chain.

title: Billionaire Pharma Heir Leaves Riches to Mystery Charity
summary: One of the billionaire heirs behind French drugmaker Ipsen SA has left his $4 billion fortune to a Luxembourg charity and an offshore trust, clouding the outlook for the company’s long-term control and ownership.

title: Digital Euro Clears Key Hurdle to Rollout in EU Parliament
summary: European Union lawmakers cleared the way for talks on the legal framework that will underpin the digital euro, a project deemed vital to the bloc’s monetary independence.

title: Tech Stock Selloff Spreads to Europe | The Opening Trade 6/23/2026
summary:
A selloff cascaded across global markets as investors turned jittery about high-flying tech stocks and frothy valuations. South Korea’s Kospi Index plunged 10% from a record high.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Rout Continues as Tech Selloff Hits Stocks | The Pulse 6/23/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Ludovic Subran, Allianz CIO & Chief Economist; Victoria Honeyman, University of Leeds Professor of British Politics; Antony Jenkins, 10x Banking CEO & Founder (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: New York City is living through the noisiest Congressional primary season in a generation.

summary: Investors are focusing on AI risks and potential interest rate hikes.

title: ECB’s Lane Sees Danger Inflation Will Top 2% for Some Time
summary: European Central Bank officials are facing the risk that inflation will hover above their goal “for quite some time,” according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

title: Gold Slumps as Tech Selloff Reverberates Through Global Markets
summary: Gold slumped toward $4,000 an ounce and silver plunged as much as 5% as a broad selloff swept through financial markets.

title: Bristow Is Said to Near Berry Aviation Deal in Government Push
summary: Bristow Group Inc. is nearing an acquisition of privately held Berry Aviation Inc. as it seeks to expand in the government services sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: High Frequency Traders Expand Push Into Currency Options Market
summary: High-speed trading firms are making fresh inroads into the currency options market, with IMC Trading and Susquehanna International Group expanding globally and Optiver Holding pursuing more complex offerings.

title: Euro Hits August Low as Lagarde’s Rates Talk Diverges From Fed
summary: The euro fell to its lowest level since August after weak economic data and dovish comments from European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde prompted traders to pare back their bets on interest-rate hikes.

title: Yen Intervention Risk Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
summary: Currency traders are on high alert for intervention after further weakness in the yen and a call between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

title: Sturgeon’s Estranged Husband Gets 5 Years for Embezzlement
summary: The Scottish National Party’s former chief executive — who’s also the estranged husband of ex-leader Nicola Sturgeon — was sentenced to five years and three months in prison after admitting to embezzling more than £400,000 ($529,000).

title: Monte Paschi CEO Lovaglio Says He Welcomes Interest in Bank
summary: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio said he’s pleased with the competing proposals for his bank as they show the value created in its turnaround, and plans to assess all options in the interest of stakeholders.

title: AllianzGI Gets $744 Million for Latest APAC Private Credit Fund
summary: Allianz Global Investors secured $744 Million in commitments for the first close of its latest private credit fund targeting investments in the Asia Pacific, underscoring sustained investor interest for the asset class in the region.
